examples: This concept does not imply that business is benevolent or that consumer satisfaction is given priority over profit in a company.
The almighty and benevolent God is strongly disconfirmed for residents of Haiti, yet they continue to believe that God is good and all powerful.
He's devotion keeps my girls on a benevolent leash, one that hangs quietly at their side as they trot along but occasionally yanks them back to safety and solid ground.
The Port Authority Police Benevolent Association on Monday denied that its officers' rescue efforts were unsafe.
During McDermott's last eight years, he was the union delegate for the Patrolman's Benevolent Association.
